pi_items.source: Calshot coordinates (50.8183°N, -1.3050°W) from latitude.to and Bridge Command geographic data for Southampton Water approach. Task bounds of lat 50.800-50.860 contain Calshot which is the reference point mentioned in task description.

Southampton Water center coordinates (50.8537°N, -1.3418°W). Task bounds capture the approach channel area of Southampton Water. Bounds are consistent with geographic reality of Southampton Water estuary approach.
Southampton Water center longitude from latitude.to. Task bounds of -1.340 to -1.280 represent the eastern portion of Southampton Water approach, consistent with geographic reality.
Calshot coordinates and Southampton Water geography. Bounds capture the width of the approach channel area.

COLREGS (Convention on the International Regulations for Preventing Collisions at Sea, 1972) documentation from IMO and maritime training resources. Rule 9 covers Narrow Channels, Rule 6 covers Safe Speed, Rule 34 covers Maneuvering and Warning Signals. All other terms (starboard, overtaking, impeding) are standard maritime vocabulary found in COLREGS.
This is a requirement specification, not a factual claim about existing data. No verification possible before task execution.
This is a directory path specification for output, not a factual claim about existing data. Directory will be created by task agent.
This is a file path specification for output, not a factual claim about existing data. File will be created by task agent.

pi_items.metadata_value: Setting='Solent' (from task description, not metadata field)
pi_items.source: Bridge Command github.com/bridgecommand/bc-world repository shows available worlds in 5.x: River, SantaCatalina, SimpleEstuary, SimpleEstuaryMultiResolution. 'Solent' is NOT a valid world setting in Bridge Command. This contradicts the task description requirement to 'Configure environment.ini: Setting=\'Solent\''. This is a critical error in the task as described.

privileged_info_summary: The metadata geographic bounds (lat 50.800-50.860, long -1.340--1.280) correctly represent the Southampton Water approach channel area, containing Calshot at coordinates 50.8183°N, -1.3050°W. The required keywords (Rule 9, Rule 34, Rule 6, starboard, overtaking, impeding) are authentic COLREGS maritime regulations and standard nautical terms. However, the task description contains a critical error: it requires Setting='Solent' as a Bridge Command world, but Solent is not a valid world in Bridge Command. Available worlds are: River, SantaCatalina, SimpleEstuary, and SimpleEstuaryMultiResolution.
